calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-21-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall appeared by Zoom for Kim Schwarcz (Administrator).
Sean Kenney appeared by Zoom on behalf of Myers Kenney PC.

The Court noted that there is no proof of service of the supplemental declaration. Ms. Hall confirmed that there is no proof of service, stated that she is not asking for more fees than what was in the petition, and that a fee declaration was done to reflect the work that was done at a specific time and that it separates out ordinary and extraordinary services. Ms. Hall also stated that the fee declaration was noticed. The Court noted that a proof of service on the declaration is needed, or it is considered ex parte. Ms. Hall stated that the Sep. 15, 2017 research entry was for figuring out postal codes for the process of serving in Israel and that it took more time, that the Oct. 3, 2017 and the Oct. 10, 2017 entries refer to the Individual Retirement Accounts ("IRA"), that they initially thought that the IRAs had no beneficiary designations and were part of the estate, that they then found that there were designated beneficiaries who were in Israel and that they spent an amount of time trying to get the IRAs to the beneficiaries. Ms. Hall also stated that she stipulates that the 9/7/17 and the 9/15/17 through 10/3/17 entries are ordinary services with the exception of the three entries she spoke of. Mr. Kenny addressed the Probate Examiner concerns regarding ordinary versus extraordinary services and stated that he would like to stand on the extraordinary services that were submitted in exhibit B. The Amended Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jul-17-2019 Continued To Jul-31-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall for Kim Schwarcz (Administrator).

Ms. Hall stated that she has responses to the Probate Examiner's notes in her office and has a declaration prepared, that she needs to file it, and that it is not signed, but it is drafted and has been sent over to Ms. Schwarcz. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to July 31, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Hall is to have her document filed by July 24, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Appearances: Mercy Hall for Kim Schwarcz (Administrator).

Ms. Hall stated that she submitted the bank statements this morning, that she has prepared a declaration in response to the Examiner's notes and that she should have it all filed within the next week. The Petition for Final Distribution is continued to July 17, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Hall is to have her documents filed no later than July 10th.

Ms. Hall stated that the final petition was e-filed and that it is pending. Status on close of estate is continued to February 27, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or Further Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Continued To Feb-13-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall for Kim Schwarcz (Administrator).

Ms. Hall stated that she just got the fee allocation agreement last week, that the petition is with her Client for signing and that she will file a petition to terminate the estate. Status on close of estate is continued to February 13, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Hall is to file either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration by February 6, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or Further Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Dec-05-2018 Continued To Jan-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall for Administrator.

The Court stated that it is looking for a petition for final distribution. Ms. Hall stated that she had to make a few changes to the accounting, that there is a fee allocation situation because prior Counsel's firm was involved prior to that firm dissolving and that they are asking for guidance from the Court. The Court informed Ms. Hall that it wants an agreement from Counsel as to how they will allocate fees. Status on filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is continued to January 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or Further Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Nov-07-2018 Continued To Dec-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall for Administrator.

Ms. Hall stated that the Decedent died while living in Israel, that the Decedent was a resident of Israel, that she received a letter indicating that she had to show how distribution was going to be done and that distribution is governed by Israeli law. Ms. Hall also stated that she got the order from Israel, that she is working on the petition for final distribution and that she can have it done within 30-days. Status on filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is continued to December 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
